name: La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route
name_original: La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Ruta circular
description: This moderate 25.7-mile mountain biking route near La Granadella offers scenic views and a good challenge for experienced riders.
region_intro: Mountain biking around La Granadella offers diverse terrain and stunning natural beauty on the Costa Blanca. The region is characterized by rugged limestone cliffs, dense pine forests, and Mediterranean vegetation, providing a varied landscape for cyclists. Trails often feature elevation gains, traversing the Granadella mountain massif and offering panoramic views. This area provides a mix of forest trails and singletrack experiences for various skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around La Granadella The most popular mountain bike trail is Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia, a 18.6 miles (30.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 29 minutes to complete. This route explores the natural surroundings of the Ebro reservoir. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route, a moderate 25.7 miles (41.4 km) path. It winds through varied landscapes, connecting several local towns and natural areas. Local mountain bikers also love the Viewpoint of the Ebro Lands loop from La Granadella, a 24.5 miles (39.4 km) trail leading through scenic viewpoints, often completed in about 3 hours 57 minutes. Mountain biking around La Granadella is defined by rugged limestone cliffs, thick pine forests, and diverse Mediterranean vegetation. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate forest trails to more challenging routes with significant elevation. The routes in La Granadella are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 3.6 stars from 5 reviews. More than 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore La Granadella's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in La Granadella?
A: La Granadella offers nearly 30 mountain bike trails, catering to various skill levels. You'll find a mix of moderate and challenging routes, with 21 moderate and 8 difficult trails available.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in La Granadella?
A: The terrain in La Granadella is diverse, characterized by rugged limestone cliffs, dense pine forests, and Mediterranean vegetation. Trails often feature significant elevation gains, traversing the Granadella mountain massif and offering panoramic views. You'll encounter both forest trails and singletrack experiences.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in La Granadella?
A: While La Granadella is known for its moderate to difficult trails, with 21 moderate and 8 difficult routes, there are currently no trails explicitly classified as easy. Many routes require a moderate level of effort and good fitness, often involving elevation gains.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in La Granadella?
A: Yes, La Granadella features several excellent circular mountain bike routes. A popular option is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e946065325/la-granadella-bovera-palma-d-ebre-bellaguarda-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 41.4 km path that winds through varied landscapes. Another is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e955890171/pantano-de-la-palma-de-ebro-circular-por-la-cueva-de-hospital-santa-llucia" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pantano de la Palma de Ebro – circular por la cueva de Hospital Santa Llúcia&lt;/a&gt;, which explores the natural surroundings of the Ebro reservoir.

Q: What are some scenic mountain bike routes with good viewpoints in La Granadella?
A: For stunning views, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20144543" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Ebro Lands loop from La Granadella&lt;/a&gt;, a 39.4 km trail leading through scenic vistas. The region itself is known for its numerous viewpoints, especially along the PR CV-354 trail or above the Granadella Castle, offering breathtaking panoramas of the coastline and Mediterranean Sea.

Q: What natural features can I see along the mountain bike trails in La Granadella?
A: You'll encounter spectacular Mediterranean landscapes, including rugged limestone cliffs, thick pine forests, and diverse flora. The Granadella Forest Park is home to various plant species, and you might spot wildlife like wild boar or foxes. A notable natural attraction is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5098390" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pine of la Palma d'Ebre&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or caves to explore near the mountain bike routes?
A: Yes, you can find interesting natural formations. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4584414" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cadolla dels Forats Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight in the area. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5086443" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Llúcia Hospital Cave&lt;/a&gt; offers an intriguing natural monument to explore.

Q: What historical landmarks can I visit while mountain biking in La Granadella?
A: La Granadella has a rich history. You can visit the ruins of the 18th-century Granadella Castle, a coastal fortification offering great views. Other historical elements include ancient ochre mines, traditional lime kilns, and unique fishing structures called 'Les Pesqueres'. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3020324" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Adar Arch&lt;/a&gt; is another historical site worth noting.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in La Granadella?
A: The mountain bike routes in La Granadella are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 3.6 stars. Over 200 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the stunning natural beauty, diverse landscapes, and the mix of forest trails and singletrack experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year to go mountain biking in La Granadella?
A: La Granadella, being on the Costa Blanca, generally offers pleasant conditions for mountain biking for much of the year. Spring and autumn typically provide the most comfortable temperatures. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon rides are advisable during peak season.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. La Granadella offers 8 difficult mountain bike routes for experienced riders seeking a challenge. One such route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10421714" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Set River – Granyena de les Garrigues loop from La Granadella&lt;/a&gt;, an extensive 84.6 km trail with significant elevation gains, perfect for those with advanced riding skills and good fitness.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the mountain biking areas in La Granadella?
A: La Granadella and the surrounding Xàbia area offer various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. The picturesque Cala de la Granadella itself has facilities, and nearby towns provide a wider selection of services for visitors.
tips: From the top of the town of Granadella, we will find this viewpoint that, as the name says, is facing the Tierra del Ebro. We will have a beautiful picture with views of Montsant, the Sierra de Cavalls, the Sierra de la Fatarella, the nuclear power plant of Ascó and the mountains of Tierras del Ebro.

Parish church of the town of Bovera, in the Garrigues region. At present, it resembles a baroque temple, but belongs to the transitional Romanesque. We cannot pass by the church without taking a walk through the quiet and welcoming town of Bovera.

Located on the outskirts of the town of Palma d'Ebre in the direction of the reservoir, we find this impressive white pine, over 400 years old and with the largest trunk in all of Catalonia.

At their feet, a millstone, they serve as tables or chairs in case we want to recover energy with a snack.

If we take a hot day, the shade of this pine will be great.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route, and is it suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This mountain biking route is classified as moderate, corresponding to an STS S2 technical difficulty. It requires good fitness and advanced riding skills due to varied and somewhat technical terrain, including small obstacles like roots and stones, uneven surfaces, and potentially tight turns. It is not recommended for beginners.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is varied and technical, consistent with an STS S2 rating. Riders can expect sections with small obstacles such as roots and stones, uneven surfaces, and potentially some tight turns. Advanced riding skills are necessary to navigate these conditions comfortably.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this mountain biking route?
faqs.answer: The estimated duration for completing this 41.4-kilometer mountain biking route is approximately 3 hours and 45 minutes (13,482 seconds), assuming a moderate pace and good riding conditions.

faqs.question: Where does the route start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: While a specific trailhead is not detailed, the route is a circular one connecting La Granadella, Bovera, Palma d'Ebre, and Bellaguarda. Parking is generally available in or near these towns, which serve as potential starting points for the loop. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or viewpoints can I expect to see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you can enjoy several points of interest. Keep an eye out for the Viewpoint of the Ebro Lands, offering scenic vistas. You'll also pass by the Church of San José de Bovera and the notable Pine of la Palma d'Ebre.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to ride this route?
faqs.answer: The route is located in a region known for its natural beauty. Generally, spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for mountain biking, avoiding the extreme heat of summer and potential colder, wetter conditions in winter. These seasons also provide optimal conditions for enjoying the natural landscapes.

faqs.question: Is the La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this particular route are not provided, it's common practice in natural areas to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other trail users. Always ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to use this trail?
faqs.answer: There are no specific permits or entrance fees mentioned for accessing the La Granadella - Bovera - Palma d'Ebre - Bellaguarda — Circular route. It is generally open for public use.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several local paths. You will traverse parts of the Camí del Pi de Cal Pino, Camí del Pi de cal Pino, camí vell de la Bisbal, Plaça de l'Església, Camí de la Granadella, and Camí de les Peces.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or amenities near the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through several towns including La Granadella, Bovera, Palma d'Ebre, and Bellaguarda, you can expect to find basic amenities such as cafes, shops, or restaurants in these settlements. It's a good idea to plan your stops in advance, especially in smaller villages.

faqs.question: What should I bring for a mountain bike ride on this route?
faqs.answer: Given the route's moderate difficulty and 41.4 km length, it's essential to bring plenty of water, snacks, a repair kit for your bike, and appropriate safety gear including a helmet. Sun protection and layers of clothing are also recommended, depending on the season.

waypoints.name: Church of San José de Bovera
